record.bridgyOriginalText:
One of the side effects of #generativeAI in #SoftwareDevelopment is that a colleague who makes heavy use of it submits merge requests that are often way beyond the task scope. Fix a translated label in certain part of the app? Fix all the appearances of the label across the app. Add a new document type to the document entity? Refactor the entire mechanism to handle document types. Small tickets grow, and huge changes can be released untested if the reviewer doesn't let QA know.
#llm #slop

images.alt: Detailed fantasy illustration styled as an architectural blueprint on light graph paper with visible pencil construction lines and faint grid. The composition is split vertically into order versus chaos. Left panel features a poised, ethereal woman as the Libra zodiac symbol: fair skin, long straight hair, wearing ornate white gown with gold filigree and celestial motifs, standing calmly with perfectly level golden scales of justice. Floating elements include antique hourglasses, torn parchment with Latin phrases such as 'Astra inclinant, sed non obligant' (The stars incline us, they do not bind us) and 'Chaos reigns', broken crystals, and open books. Right panel shows the chaotic counterpart: the woman’s form is breaking apart, her torso and limbs filled with voids and screaming ghostly faces bursting outward in swirling clouds of pink, turquoise, and lavender mist. Her scales are shattered and tilted, dripping dark ink-like substance. Additional broken hourglasses and debris litter the base where the two halves meet in a jagged, crumbling divide. The artwork combines precise technical drafting lines, soft pastel watercolor shading, and intricate symbolic details to portray the tension between harmony and entropy.

images.alt:
To the moon and back is far too small
for the distance my heart travels to reach you.
Across this lifetime, through the echoes of the last,
and into the shimmering dawns of futures yet unborn,
our love keeps finding its way home.

We are written in the quiet language of the stars—
a story older than breath,
traced in constellations that remember our names.
Every time the universe expands,
it makes more room for us,
for the gravity that pulls our souls together
again and again.

In every life, I find you.
In every life, I choose you.
In every life, I love you
beyond the borders of time,
beyond the rise and fall of worlds,
beyond the final turning of the cosmic wheel.

Our love is meant to be—
not by chance,
but by destiny written in stardust
and sealed in eternity.
